tasks.name: Register for the earliest lead climbing safety workshop in Colorado under $80
tasks.description: Find upcoming education events, filter to lead climbing safety workshops located in Colorado costing $80 or less, sort by date, and complete registration for the earliest qualifying workshop.
steps: Navigate to the website homepage.
Click on the top navigation menu item labeled "Events" and select "Workshops" from the dropdown.
In the workshop type filter on the left sidebar, select the checkbox for "Lead Climbing Safety."
In the location filter section, open the state dropdown and select "Colorado" as the state.
In the price filter, set the maximum price field to "80" and apply the filter so only workshops priced at $80 or less are shown.
Open the sort dropdown above the workshop list and choose "Date - Soonest First" to sort by the earliest upcoming date.
Click on the first workshop card in the sorted results list (the earliest lead climbing safety workshop in Colorado under $80).
On the workshop detail page, click the "Register" button.

On the registration form, enter a sample name (e.g., "Taylor Morgan"), email (e.g., "taylor@example.com"), and membership ID (e.g., "CO12345"), select the payment option "Pay at Venue" if available, and click the "Complete Registration" button.

tasks.name: Purchase a 1-year professional routesetter membership under $120 with auto-renew turned off
tasks.description: Go to membership plans, compare options, select the Professional Routesetter plan with a 1-year term priced under $120, disable auto-renewal, and complete the on-site payment form.
steps: Navigate to the website homepage.
Click on the main navigation item labeled "Membership" and select "Plans & Pricing" from the dropdown.
On the plans page, click the "Compare Plans" link or tab to view details for different membership types.
In the comparison table or list, locate the "Professional Routesetter" membership and click its "Select" or "Choose Plan" button.
In the duration or billing options, select the "1-Year" term that is shown with a price under $120.
Locate the "Auto-Renew" toggle or checkbox for the membership and switch it to the OFF position (so renewal will not occur automatically).
Click the "Continue" or "Join Now" button to proceed to the checkout or payment page.

On the checkout form, fill in the required personal fields such as full name (e.g., "Alex Rivera"), email (e.g., "alex.routesetter@example.com"), and billing address including city (e.g., "Boulder"), state, and ZIP code.

In the payment section, choose the "Credit/Debit Card" option, enter sample card details (e.g., card number "4111111111111111", expiration "12/28", CVV "123"), and click the "Submit Payment" or "Complete Purchase" button.

tasks.name: Bookmark 3 advanced anchor-building articles published after January 1, 2023
tasks.description: Search the resource library for anchor-building content, filter to advanced-level articles published after January 1, 2023, sort by recency, and add the first three matching articles to your reading list.
steps: Navigate to the website homepage.
Click on the main navigation item labeled "Resources" and choose "Articles" from the dropdown menu.
In the articles search bar, type "anchor building" and press Enter to run the search.
In the skill level filter section, select the "Advanced" checkbox so only advanced articles are shown.
In the date or publication filter, set the start date to "January 1, 2023" so it shows only articles published after that date, then apply the filter.
Use the sort dropdown above the results list to select "Most Recent" so the newest articles appear first.

From the filtered results list, click on the first article title to open its detail page, click the "Save to Reading List" or "Bookmark" button, then use the browser Back function or site breadcrumb to return to the results; repeat this open-and-save process for the second and third articles in the list so that three advanced, post-2023 anchor-building articles are added to your reading list.

tasks.name: Add 2 Denver-area youth-program gyms within 20 miles to your Favorite Gyms list
tasks.description: Use the gym directory to find member gyms within 20 miles of Denver, Colorado that offer youth programs, sort by distance, and add the first two gyms shown to your Favorite Gyms list.
steps: Navigate to the website homepage.
Click on the navigation item labeled "Gym Directory" or "Find a Gym" to open the directory page.
In the location search field, type "Denver, CO" and click the search icon or press Enter.
In the distance or radius filter, select or enter "Within 20 miles" to limit the search area around Denver.
Open the amenities or programs filter and check the option labeled "Youth Programs" or "Youth Climbing" so only gyms with youth offerings are shown.
Use the sort dropdown to choose "Distance - Closest First" so gyms are listed by proximity to Denver.
Click on the first gym card in the filtered list to open its detail page, then click the "Add to Favorite Gyms" or "Save" button to add it to your favorites.

Return to the gym results list (using Back or a "Return to Results" link), click on the second gym card in the list, and on its detail page click the "Add to Favorite Gyms" or "Save" button to add the second gym to your favorites.

tasks.name: Apply to a head routesetter job in the Pacific region with salary at least $55,000 using a saved profile
tasks.description: Search the job board for head routesetter positions in the Pacific region offering salaries of $55,000 or more, sort by newest postings, and submit an application to the first qualifying job using an existing saved profile.
steps: Navigate to the website homepage.
Click on the navigation item labeled "Careers" or "Job Board" to open the job listings page.
In the job role or position filter, select "Head Routesetter" as the role.
In the region or location filter, select the "Pacific" region from the available options.
In the salary or compensation filter, set the minimum salary field to "55000" and apply the filter so only jobs with salaries of $55,000 or higher are displayed.
Use the sort dropdown above the job list to select "Posted - Newest First" so the most recently posted roles appear at the top.
Click on the first job listing in the filtered, sorted results to open the job detail page.
On the job detail page, click the "Apply with Saved Profile" or similar button instead of any upload-based option.

In the application modal or form, choose the saved profile labeled "Default Professional Profile" (or the first available profile) from the dropdown and click the "Submit Application" or "Send Application" button.

tasks.name: Register a youth climber for the cheapest May 2025 bouldering competition within 100 miles of Seattle
tasks.description: Find bouldering competitions scheduled in May 2025 within 100 miles of Seattle, sort by registration fee, and register a youth participant for the competition with the lowest registration price using the basic tier without add-ons.
steps: Navigate to the website homepage.
Click on the "Events" navigation item and select "Competitions" from the dropdown.
In the discipline filter on the competitions page, select "Bouldering" so only bouldering competitions are shown.
In the date range filter, set the start date to "May 1, 2025" and the end date to "May 31, 2025", then apply the filter.
In the location search or filter, type "Seattle, WA" and set the radius option to "Within 100 miles" to limit competitions to that area.
Use the sort dropdown above the competition list to select "Registration Fee - Low to High" so the cheapest competitions appear first.
Click on the first competition card in the sorted list (the least expensive qualifying bouldering competition in May 2025 near Seattle).
On the competition detail page, click the "Register Participant" or "Sign Up" button.

In the registration form, enter the participant name (e.g., "Jordan Lee"), select the age category as "Youth", leave any optional add-on checkboxes (such as extra training sessions or merchandise) unselected, choose the registration tier that is clearly labeled as the lowest price (e.g., "Basic"), and click the "Complete Registration" button.

tasks.name: Enroll in an intermediate coaching course rated 4.5+ stars and under 8 weeks, then add it to your learning plan
tasks.description: Browse education courses, filter to intermediate-level courses shorter than 8 weeks with ratings of at least 4.5 stars, sort by highest rating, enroll in the top-ranked course, and add it to your personal learning plan.
steps: Navigate to the website homepage.
Click on the "Education" navigation item and select "Courses" from the dropdown menu.
In the level filter on the courses page, select "Intermediate" so only intermediate courses are displayed.
In the duration filter, select the option such as "Up to 8 weeks" or set a maximum length of "8 weeks" and apply the filter.
In the rating filter, choose to show only courses with a rating of "4.5 stars & up" or enter a minimum rating value of "4.5".
Use the sort dropdown above the course list to select "Rating - High to Low" so the highest-rated courses appear first.

Click on the first course card in the filtered, sorted list to open its detail page, then click the "Enroll" button and, if a format choice is offered, select the "Self-paced" option before confirming enrollment.
On the post-enrollment or confirmation page, click the "Add to My Learning Plan" or "Save to Learning Plan" button to include this course in your personal learning plan.

tasks.name: Volunteer for an online evening safety standards committee and select at least 2 interest areas
tasks.description: Find a safety standards committee that meets online in the evenings, open its detail page, and submit a volunteer interest form selecting at least two specific areas of interest.
steps: Navigate to the website homepage.
Click on the "About" navigation item and select "Committees & Volunteering" from the dropdown.
In the committee category filter on the committees page, select "Safety Standards" to show only safety-related committees.
In the meeting format filter, choose "Online" or "Virtual" to restrict the list to committees that meet remotely.
In the meeting time filter, select the option labeled "Evenings (after 6 PM)" or similar to focus on evening committees.
From the filtered list, click on a committee that matches the Safety Standards category, online format, and evening meeting time to open its detail page.
On the committee detail page, click the "Volunteer" or "Express Interest" button to open the sign-up form.

In the volunteer form, fill in required information such as name (e.g., "Morgan Patel") and email (e.g., "morgan@example.com"), then in the "Areas of Interest" multi-select field choose at least two options—for example, "Gym Safety Inspections" and "Training Guidelines"—before clicking the "Submit Interest" button.
